mechanism_narrative: A lower federal funds rate relative to recent history reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows, mechanically increasing present values and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC between FEDFUNDS level and 20-day S&P 500 forward returns remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A rising unemployment rate over a three-month window signals deteriorating household income expectations and weakening aggregate demand, which causes negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 60-day forward returns in more than 55% of instances when UNRATE has risen more than 0.2 percentage points over the prior 3 months over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous acceleration in retail sales and industrial production captures a broadening demand recovery where consumption and manufacturing momentum align, forecasting positive S&P 500 returns because earnings revisions follow broad-based demand strength rather than narrow consumption alone.
failure_condition: The bivariate interaction term between RSAFS and INDPRO produces a t-statistic below 1.5 in predicting 20-day S&P 500 returns over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in consumer prices during near-zero policy rate periods signal reflation that reduces real debt burdens and supports nominal revenue growth, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days because monetary policy remains accommodative and does not choke the recovery.
failure_condition: When FEDFUNDS is below 0.5%, month-over-month CPIAUCSL increases coincide with negative 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of instances over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month acceleration in industrial production signals expanding manufacturing capacity utilization and operating leverage, which drives positive earnings surprises and positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive momentum in INDPRO fails to correlate with positive 5-day S&P 500 returns with Rank IC greater than 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts falling below the 700 thousand unit threshold signals deteriorating construction activity and downstream demand for durable goods and materials, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: When HOUST is below 700, S&P 500 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of occurrences over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Jointly rising nonfarm payrolls and a falling unemployment rate confirm broad-based labor market tightening rather than statistical noise, predicting positive S&P 500 returns over 60 days as consumption expectations firm and wage-driven revenue growth materializes.
failure_condition: The interaction of PAYEMS growth and UNRATE decline produces a Rank IC below 0.03 for 60-day S&P 500 returns over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When industrial production declines for two consecutive months while the federal funds rate is above 1 percent, the combination of slowing supply and restrictive monetary policy generates negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The condition of consecutive INDPRO declines with FEDFUNDS above 1% produces positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of triggered episodes over a 3-year rolling window.
